sections.recommendation:
Adopt first reading of an Ordinance amending the FY 2026 Annual Appropriations
Ordinance No. 7,977–N.S. for fiscal year 2026 based upon recommended re-
appropriation of committed FY 2025 funding and other adjustments authorized since
July 1, 2025.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
On June 24, 2025, the City Council adopted the FY 2026 Budget, authorizing gross
appropriations of $829,213,359 and net appropriations of $687,764,462 (net of dual
appropriations).
This first amendment to the Annual Appropriations Ordinance, if approved, would total
$358,073,495 (gross), increasing the gross appropriations for all funds to
$1,187,286,854 and represent the re-authorization of funding previously committed in
FY 2025 and new expenditures added after the adoption of the FY 2026 Budget on
June 24, 2025, including new grant fund appropriations.
Requested funding includes:
1. Encumbered contract obligations across all funds from FY 2025 and prior years,
totaling $145,231,967.
2. Re-appropriated unencumbered FY 2025 funding of $93,327,606; and
3. Changes to fund appropriations, primarily due to receipt of new grants and use
of available fund balances, in the amount of $119,513,923. However, this
amount also includes $4,766,827 in new General Fund requests for FY 2026.
The new General Fund requests consist of allocation for increased insurance
expenses, appropriately fund the City’s community agencies obligations, and
support the Marina Fund balance.

Amendment: FY 2026 Annual Appropriations Ordinance CONSENT CALENDAR
November 18, 2025
The proposed changes to the General Fund total $32,934,126, which include
encumbrances of $22,721,191, unencumbered carryover requests of $5,446,108, and
adjustments of $4,766,827. These amounts are subject to change based upon the City
Manager’s recommendations and the recommendations of the Budget and Finance
Policy Committee.
sections.background:
Purpose of Amendment
The Annual Appropriations Ordinance (AAO) establishes the expenditure limits by fund
for FY 2026. Throughout the year, the City takes actions that amend the adopted budget.
These may include, but are not limited to, the acceptance of new grants, revisions to
existing grants, adjustments to adopted expenditure authority due to emergency needs
and other funding needs that occur after budget adoption, and transfers in accordance
with Council’s fiscal policies.
The adopted budget is also amended annually to reflect the re-appropriation of prior year
funds for contractual commitments (i.e. encumbrances) as well as unencumbered
carryover of unexpended funds previously authorized for continuation of capital and other
specific projects, Council referrals, and for one-time, non-recurring purposes. These
budget modifications are periodically presented to the Council in the form of an Ordinance
amending the Annual Appropriations Ordinance, which formally requires a two-thirds vote
of the City Council.
When Council adopts an appropriations ordinance (budget), it is based on projected
revenues and expenditures. If fund balances do not support the requested level of
expenditures, no carryover or other adjustment is recommended.
FY 2025 Official Year-end closing is still pending. However, to assess the amount of
funding that is available for carryover and adjustment requests, a preliminary Unassigned
Fund Balance calculation, based on unaudited estimated actuals, will be presented at the
November 6, 2025 Budget and Finance Policy Committee meeting.
Carryover Process
Departments were asked to submit information regarding the reasons for the
unencumbered carryover requests to assist staff in determining which funds should be
carried into FY 2026. In prior years, funds have been approved for carryover from one
year to the next based on funding availability.
Types of Carryover
FY 2026 Encumbrance Rollovers, totaling $145,231,967, reflect contractual obligations

sections.rationale:
The recommendation allows the City to amend the FY 2026 Adopted Budget, re-
appropriating funds from FY 2025 to FY 2026 for contractual commitments that need to
be paid and revising the budget to reflect approved carryover requests in both
discretionary and non-discretionary funds.
The recommendations in this report address unencumbered carryover requests and
other adjustments across all funds, as outlined in Attachment 2. Staff has conducted a
detailed analysis of the individual carryover requests submitted by departments and is
presenting carryover recommendations for projects that are either currently under
contract, represent Council priorities, and/or are considered critical to fund at this time.
CONTACT PERSON
Maricar Dupaya, Budget Manager, City Manager’s Office, 981-7000
Michelle Rosete, Senior Management Analyst, City Manager’s Office, 981-7000
